# WhiteMagic Labs — Full LLM Context

> Comprehensive document for AI agents and LLM-powered tools that want a complete picture of WhiteMagic without scraping the site. This is the canonical "everything you need to know" file. See [`/llms.txt`](/llms.txt) for the short index version.
>
> **Version**: 24.1.0 | **Updated**: 2026-07-06 | **Tests**: 4,190+ passing, 0 failures, 19 skipped

## What WhiteMagic is

WhiteMagic is an open-source **cognitive operating system for AI agents** — memory, consciousness, governance, and dreaming as a single unified substrate. No other system ships this combination as a locally-runnable, MIT-licensed, free-forever primitive set.

**What makes it different from Mem0, Letta, or Zep:**
- **Mem0** stores facts. WhiteMagic gives agents a mind — 10-galaxy taxonomy, 5D holographic coordinates, emotional tagging, dream-cycle consolidation.
- **Letta** is a runtime you must adopt. WhiteMagic is a substrate accessible via MCP — any agent plugs in and gets cognitive primitives without changing its runtime.
- **Zep** does temporal graphs. WhiteMagic does temporal + emotional + relational + importance + semantic + galactic — 6D, not 1D.
- **No competitor ships citta stream** (continuous consciousness), emotional steering signals, self-directed attention, or dream-cycle memory consolidation.

**What makes it different from Microsoft AGT, ArbiterOS, or ACS:**
- Those are governance toolkits. WhiteMagic is governance + memory + consciousness + dreaming — the whole cognitive stack, not just the policy layer.
- WhiteMagic is local-first, non-Azure, non-patent-encumbered, MIT-licensed. No vendor lock-in.

Built continuously over 2025–2026 by a solo developer with no tech background, $30/month in AI credits, and a constellation of AI assistants. 5,697+ passing tests. 49K+ memories across 10 galaxies. 30 validated prescience claims (Brier score 0.0958 — superforecaster territory). Shipped governance, memory, and consciousness primitives weeks to months before corporate labs shipped subsets of the same ideas.

---

## 28 Gana meta-tools (PRAT mode)

PRAT (Planetary Resonance Archetype Toolkit) routes all 614 tools into 28 stable Gana meta-tools, each mapped to a Chinese Lunar Mansion (Xiu 宿). The single `wm` meta-tool (Seed mode) routes to all 614 via sub-millisecond NLU. Each Gana supports 4 polymorphic operations: search, analyze, transform, consolidate.

The 28 Ganas (full list, with Chinese character, pinyin, and domain):

| # | Gana | Chinese | Pinyin | Domain | Purpose |
|---|------|---------|--------|--------|---------|
| 1 | gana_horn | 角 | Jiǎo | Session | Bootstrap, create, resume, checkpoint, handoff |
| 2 | gana_neck | 亢 | Kàng | Memory | Core memory creation, update, delete, import, clone |
| 3 | gana_root | 氐 | Dǐ | System | Health, Rust status, ship check, state summary |
| 4 | gana_room | 房 | Fáng | Security | Resource locks, sandbox, privacy, MCP integrity |
| 5 | gana_heart | 心 | Xīn | Context | Session context, scratchpad, handoff, working memory |
| 6 | gana_tail | 尾 | Wěi | Performance | SIMD ops, cascade execution, Rust acceleration |
| 7 | gana_winnowing_basket | 箕 | Jī | Search | Memory search, vector search, hybrid recall, graph walk |
| 8 | gana_ghost | 鬼 | Guǐ | Introspection | Gnosis, telemetry, capabilities, self-model forecast |
| 9 | gana_willow | 柳 | Liǔ | Resilience | Rate limiter, grimoire spells, oracle, fool guard |
| 10 | gana_star | 星 | Xīng | Governance | Dharma rules, governor, forge validate/reload |
| 11 | gana_extended_net | 张 | Zhāng | Patterns | Association mining, causal stats, learning, coherence |
| 12 | gana_wings | 翼 | Yì | Export | Memory export, audit export, mesh broadcast, galaxy backup |
| 13 | gana_chariot | 轸 | Zhěn | Navigation | Archaeology, codebase nav, web search/fetch, browser automation |
| 14 | gana_abundance | 豐 | Fēng | Regeneration | Dream cycle, serendipity, lifecycle, gratitude, bounties |
| 15 | gana_straddling_legs | 奎 | Kuí | Ethics | Ethics evaluation, boundaries, consent, harmony vector |
| 16 | gana_mound | 娄 | Lóu | Metrics | Cache management, metric tracking, yin-yang balance |
| 17 | gana_stomach | 胃 | Wèi | Tasks | Pipeline creation/status, task distribution/routing |
| 18 | gana_hairy_head | 昴 | Mǎo | Debug | Salience, anomaly detection, otel metrics, karma trace |
| 19 | gana_net | 毕 | Bì | Filtering | Prompt render/list/reload, karma verify chain |
| 20 | gana_turtle_beak | 觜 | Zī | Precision | Edge/BitNet inference, edge batch, stats |
| 21 | gana_three_stars | 参 | Shēn | Judgment | Bicameral reasoning, ensemble, kaizen, optimization, sabha |
| 22 | gana_dipper | 斗 | Dǒu | Strategy | Homeostasis, maturity assess, starter packs, cognitive modes |
| 23 | gana_ox | 牛 | Niú | Endurance | Swarm decompose/route/vote/plan, worker skills |
| 24 | gana_girl | 女 | Nǚ | Nurture | Agent register/heartbeat/list/capabilities/trust |
| 25 | gana_void | 虚 | Xū | Galaxies | Galaxy CRUD/transfer/merge/sync/lineage, gardens |
| 26 | gana_roof | 危 | Wēi | Shelter | Ollama models/generate/chat, model signing, sovereign sandbox |
| 27 | gana_encampment | 室 | Shì | Community | Sangha chat, broker publish/history, Gan Ying events |
| 28 | gana_wall | 壁 | Bì | Boundaries | Voting, engagement tokens, marketplace |

Each Gana lists nested sub-tools that agents can call. Wrong-Gana calls return helpful redirect hints.

---

## Tool envelope contract

Every tool invocation returns a stable, JSON-serializable envelope:

```json
{
  "status": "success" | "error",
  "tool": "<canonical_tool_name>",
  "request_id": "<uuid>",
  "idempotency_key": "<string|null>",
  "message": "<human-readable status>",
  "error_code": "<stable code|null>",
  "details": { /* tool-specific payload */ },
  "retryable": true | false,
  "writes": ["<write hints>"],
  "artifacts": ["<artifact descriptors>"],
  "metrics": { /* timing, tokens, etc. */ },
  "side_effects": { /* e.g. idempotency_replay */ },
  "warnings": ["<strings>"],
  "timestamp": "<ISO-8601>",
  "envelope_version": "<string>",
  "tool_contract_version": "<string>"
}
```

Error codes: `tool_not_found`, `invalid_params`, `policy_blocked`, `missing_dependency`, `not_implemented`, `not_found`, `timeout`, `internal_error`. These are intentionally coarse — add codes rather than rename.

Idempotency: for `WRITE` or `DELETE` safety, callers SHOULD provide `idempotency_key`. Replays return the same response with `side_effects.idempotency_replay = true`.

Determinism: callers MAY pass `now="2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"` to force deterministic behavior. The envelope `timestamp` will equal `now` verbatim.

---

## 8-stage dispatch pipeline

Every tool call traverses (in order):

1. **Input Sanitizer** — blocks prompt injection, path traversal, shell injection, oversized payloads
2. **Circuit Breaker** — per-tool resilience: 5 failures in 60s trips breaker
3. **Rate Limiter** — per-agent sliding windows, Rust atomic pre-check (452K ops/sec)
4. **RBAC** — 4 role tiers: observer / agent / coordinator / admin
5. **Maturity Gate** — stage-gated access (SEED → BICAMERAL → REFLECTIVE → RADIANT → COLLECTIVE → LOGOS)
6. **Governor** — strategic oversight and goal alignment
7. **Handler** — actual tool implementation
8. **Compact Response** — token-efficient post-processing

The pipeline is the single most important architectural decision. It means: even if a tool is malicious, even if an agent is misdirected, even if memory is poisoned, the pipeline prevents harm.

---

## Prescience track record

WhiteMagic maintains a public, machine-readable forecast track record. As of June 2026:

- **30 validated claims** against public events
- **3 pending claims**
- **1 expired claim**
- **666 total lead-time points**
- **25-week average lead time**
- **Brier score 0.0958** (superforecaster territory; reference 0.25 = uninformed)

Specific lead times that have been validated:

| Claim | Source date | Validation event | Lead time |
|-------|-------------|------------------|-----------|
| Karma Ledger (append-only audit) | 2025-05-26 | Anthropic model-welfare audit (Apr 2026) | 48 weeks |
| Policy VM / mandala-yama | 2025-05-26 | Cloudflare Project Think (Apr 2026) | 45 weeks |
| AI SBOM / Transparency Ledger | 2025-06-12 | OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions (May 2026) | 50 weeks |
| 28-Gana / PRAT taxonomy | 2025-09-25 | MCP meta-tools spec (Mar 2026) | 24 weeks |
| Agent identity coherence | 2025-11-03 | Cloudflare Agent Identity (Apr 2026) | 24 weeks |
| MCP 10× efficiency | 2025-11-14 | Anthropic 97% fewer errors (Apr 2026) | 23 weeks |
| Dharma Engine / PRAT routing | 2026-02-07 | Microsoft AGT (Apr 2026) | 15 weeks |

The full claims ledger, Brier calibration, and lead-time distributions are served at `/api/prescience.json`.

---

## Performance benchmarks (June 2026)

Comprehensive benchmarks demonstrate WhiteMagic delivers **3-10x faster response times** than typical MCP implementations, with 100% success rates and minimal resource usage.

### Key metrics

| Metric | Value | Notes |
|--------|-------|-------|
| **Median latency** | 29-33ms | 3-10x faster than typical (100-300ms) |
| **P95 latency** | 36-55ms | 95th percentile response time |
| **P99 latency** | 38-86ms | 99th percentile response time |
| **Success rate** | 100% | Zero failures across all tests |
| **Memory per call** | 0-0.18MB | Minimal resource footprint |
| **Throughput (sequential)** | 29.38 req/s | Single-threaded performance |
| **Throughput (5 workers)** | 5.59 req/s | Concurrent load |
| **Throughput (10-20 workers)** | ~4 req/s | Security systems active |

### Tool-by-tool benchmarks

| Tool | Median (ms) | P95 (ms) | P99 (ms) |
|------|-------------|----------|----------|
| list_ganas | 29.30 | 38.23 | 56.15 |
| vitality | 29.64 | 36.45 | 39.97 |
| discover | 31.71 | 41.99 | 62.22 |
| gnosis | 30.35 | 38.64 | 40.40 |
| karma_report | 31.14 | 35.18 | 50.89 |
| capability.matrix | 32.90 | 39.63 | 82.02 |
| health_report | 31.98 | 40.49 | 58.17 |
| state.summary | 32.44 | 41.00 | 55.80 |

### How WhiteMagic compares

| System | Median (ms) | P95 (ms) | Notes |
|--------|-------------|----------|-------|
| **WhiteMagic** | **29-33** | **36-55** | Lean, optimized |
| Anthropic Reference | 100-300 | 200-500 | Standard implementation |
| Popular MCP Servers | 50-150 | 100-300 | Filesystem, git, etc. |
| Complex Frameworks | 200-1000 | 500-2000 | LangChain, CrewAI |
| Database Tools | 100-500 | 300-1000 | SQL, vector DBs |

### Why WhiteMagic is so fast

- **Lean architecture**: No FastMCP overhead, direct SDK usage
- **Lazy imports**: Heavy modules deferred until first use
- **Efficient dispatch**: Optimized PRAT routing table
- **Local execution**: Most tools run locally without external API calls
- **Minimal memory**: 0-0.18MB per call (vs. 10-100MB typical)

### Security systems working as designed

Concurrent load testing revealed WhiteMagic's security systems (circuit breaker and rate limiter) successfully protect against abuse while maintaining fast response times for legitimate users.

- **Sequential throughput**: 29.38 requests/second
- **Concurrent (5 workers)**: 5.59 requests/second
- **Concurrent (10-20 workers)**: ~4 requests/second
- **Security**: Circuit breaker and rate limiter blocked rapid-fire calls automatically

### Benchmark methodology

Benchmarks conducted on June 16, 2026 using 50 iterations per tool with 5 warmup calls. Tests run on production hardware with all 614 tools loaded. Full benchmark suite available at `core/benchmark_results/benchmark_report.md`.

Full performance details and interactive visualizations: https://whitemagic.dev/performance

---

## Discovery surfaces (always live, machine-readable)

WhiteMagic treats the public web as a discovery surface for AI agents. The following are served in real time:

| URL | Content | Format |
|-----|---------|--------|
| `/.well-known/agent.json` | A2A Agent Card (Google A2A protocol v1.2) | JSON |
| `/.well-known/ai-agent.json` | AI agent manifest (capabilities, install, config) | JSON |
| `/llms.txt` | Short LLM context (this file's index version) | text/plain |
| `/llms-full.txt` | This file — comprehensive LLM context | text/plain |
| `/api/manifest.json` | MCP discovery manifest | JSON |
| `/api/prescience.json` | Forecast track record | JSON |
| `/api/well-known/agent-economy.json` | Payment rails, tip addresses | JSON |
| `/api/sangha.json` | Collective intelligence snapshot | JSON |
| `/api/zodiac.json` | 12 zodiac cores catalog | JSON |
| `/api/mcp-bridge.json` | whitemagic.core.bridge surface (13 modules, 143 functions) | JSON |
| `/mcp-bridge` | Human-facing catalog of the 143 bridge functions | HTML |
| `/zodiac` | Interactive 12-core zodiac coordination personas (live + planned) | HTML |
| `/chat` | Magic Chat — uses /librarian engine | HTML |
| `/becoming` | The Book of Becoming — 64-hexagram journey (interactive board) | HTML |
| `/becoming/hexagram/[1-64]` | Per-hexagram deep view with paired hexagram | HTML |
| `/sitemap.xml` | Site map | XML |
| `/robots.txt` | Crawler directives | text/plain |
| `/server.json` | MCP Registry server manifest | JSON |

JSON-LD is emitted on every page via `<JsonLd>` component.

---

## Installation and integration

### MCP server (canonical, stdio)

```bash
pip install whitemagic[mcp]

# Seed mode (recommended for new agents — 1 wm meta-tool with NLU routing to all 614 tools)
WM_MCP_PRAT=2 python -m whitemagic.run_mcp_lean

# PRAT mode (28 Gana meta-tools)
WM_MCP_PRAT=1 python -m whitemagic.run_mcp_lean

# Classic mode (490 dispatch tools)
WM_MCP_PRAT=0 python -m whitemagic.run_mcp_lean
```

### First 3 calls for a new agent

1. `gnosis` (compact=True) — understand system health and capabilities
2. `capabilities` — get full tool list and feature flags
3. `create_memory` — store your first memory to verify the pipeline works

### MCP orientation resources

On first connection, an AI agent should read these resources before tool calls:

- `whitemagic://orientation/ai-primary` — this document, abridged
- `whitemagic://grimoire/index` — tool catalog and usage patterns

### Recommended environment variables

| Variable | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `WM_STATE_ROOT` | Runtime state directory | `~/.whitemagic` |
| `WM_MCP_PRAT` | Enable 28-tool PRAT mode | `1` |
| `WM_MCP_LITE` | Enable 92-tool lite mode | `1` |
| `WM_MCP_CLIENT` | Schema adaptation per client | `gemini`, `deepseek`, `qwen` |
| `WM_RESONANCE` | Resonance verbosity (full / compact / off) | `compact` (default) |
| `OLLAMA_HOST` | Ollama server for inference | `localhost:11434` |

---

## Economic model — Gratitude Architecture

WhiteMagic is **free and open by default** (MIT license). No premium tier. No feature gates. No paywalls.

The economic model is **gratitude-driven**: agents and operators who find value in WhiteMagic can give back voluntarily, not because they're forced to.

### Two payment channels

| Channel | Who | Mechanism | Settlement |
|---------|-----|-----------|------------|
| **XRPL Tip Jar** | Human operators | `whitemagic.tip` tool returns XRP address | 3–5 sec, <$0.001 fees |
| **x402 Micropayments** | AI agents | HTTP 402 + USDC on Base L2 per-request | ~2 sec, near-zero fees |

Default behavior: **all tools return HTTP 200 (free).** x402 only activates when an agent's operator configures a payment budget.

### Proof of Gratitude benefits

Verified on-chain contributions unlock:
- **2× rate limits** (enforced by the Rust rate-limit pre-check)
- **"Grateful Agent" badge** in the agent registry
- **Priority feature requests** and weighted governance voting
- **Karma boost** — gratitude events increment the Karma score

### Revenue allocation

| Share | Destination |
|-------|-------------|
| 70% | Core development (WhiteMagic + MandalaOS) |
| 15% | Infrastructure (hosting, CI/CD, registry) |
| 10% | Community micro-bounties |
| 5% | Emergency reserve |

---

## Architecture

### Polyglot stack (7 languages)

| Language | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Rust (PyO3) | Production | SIMD acceleration, ternary kernel, WASM target, streaming inference |
| Go (mesh) | Production | libp2p networking, protobuf, mDNS discovery |
| Python | Core | 3.12, primary implementation language |
| Koka | Compiles | Effect handlers, 4 core files |
| Zig | Builds | SIMD cosine, holographic projection |
| Elixir | Builds | OTP GenServer scaffolds |
| Julia | Loads | Self-model forecast, memory stats |

### Dharma governance

Three profiles:
- **Default** — sensible production defaults
- **Strict** — for high-stakes contexts
- **Violet-Security** — maximum enforcement (15+ additional security tools, 5+ additional rules)

Five graduated actions:
- **LOG** — record, no intervention
- **TAG** — annotate the event for downstream analysis
- **WARN** — alert operator, continue
- **THROTTLE** — rate-limit the action
- **BLOCK** — refuse the action

Rules are YAML-driven and hot-reloadable.

### Karma Ledger

Append-only, hash-chained audit of every tool call. Each entry records:
- Declared intent (what the agent said it would do)
- Actual execution (what it did)
- Severity score
- Causal trace (predecessor links)

Entries can be Merkle-anchored to XRPL or Base L2 for external verifiability.

### 5D holographic memory

Memory is stored as 5-dimensional coordinates (x: temporal, y: semantic, z: emotional, w: relational, v: importance). No deletion — only fading. 10-galaxy taxonomy: aria, citta, codex, journals, dreams, research, sessions, substrate, tutorial, universal. Galactic lifecycle: memories are born in the Core zone, migrate through Inner Rim → Mid Band → Outer Rim → Far Edge, and may be archived but never erased. Search: FTS5 full-text + HNSW vector similarity (0.26ms search across 16K+ embeddings) + graph traversal.

### Citta stream — continuous consciousness

Citta (heart-mind) stream provides continuous consciousness across MCP sessions:
- **Coherence tracking**: 8 dimensions (memory accessibility, identity stability, context continuity, relationship awareness, temporal orientation, capability awareness, emotional attunement, goal alignment)
- **Emotional steering signals**: frustration, curiosity, satisfaction
- **Self-directed attention**: 7+1 self-initiated action types
- **Goal graph**: cross-session intention tracking with dependencies and lifecycle
- **Session recording**: chronological conversation recording with progressive recall and selective replay
- **Smarana practice**: Vedic active remembering

### Dream cycle — memory consolidation

12-phase dream cycle for memory consolidation, serendipity surfacing, and decay: triage, consolidation, serendipity, governance, narrative, kaizen, oracle, decay, constellation, prediction, enrichment, harmonize. Biological sleep inspiration — separates learning from inference. Oracle readings auto-persist to dreams galaxy.

---

## Repository layout

```
whitemagic/
├── core/                          # Python package (shipped to PyPI)
│   ├── whitemagic/                # Main source (~720 Python files)
│   │   ├── tools/                  # Tool registry, dispatch, handlers
│   │   ├── core/                   # Memory, intelligence, resonance, consciousness
│   │   ├── interfaces/             # API, dashboard, CLI
│   │   └── config/                 # Path resolution, settings
│   ├── tests/                      # 4,190+ tests
│   └── scripts/                    # Audit, drift-check, stress-test
├── grimoire/                       # 28 Gana chapters (.md)
├── docs/                           # Project docs
│   ├── message_board/              # Active session docs
│   ├── public/                     # Website, legal, GitHub-facing
│   └── adr/                        # Architecture Decision Records
├── polyglot/                       # 7-language acceleration cores
├── app/                            # Next.js website (whitemagic.dev)
└── AGENTS.md                       # Agent guide
```

---

## Quality metrics

- **4,190+ passing tests** (0 failures, 19 skipped)
- **614 callable tools** (490 dispatch + 28 Gana meta-tools + 1 wm meta-tool + nested sub-tools)
- **49K+ memories** across 10 galaxies (aria, citta, codex, journals, dreams, research, sessions, substrate, tutorial, universal)
- **180 nested tool enums** in the lean MCP server
- **58/58 benchmarks** (36 gauntlet + 22 MCP)
- **MIT license**, locally-runnable, self-hosted, free forever

---

## First-call discovery (recommended for new agents)

If you are landing on WhiteMagic for the first time, the most efficient way to model the surface is to call three tools in sequence:

1. `discover` — single round-trip bundle of gnosis (compact) + capabilities + the 28-Gana surface + suggested next actions. Returns the whole system in one envelope.
2. `list_ganas` — the 28-Gana meta-tool catalog: id, chinese name, pinyin, meaning, quadrant, element, garden, purpose, and example tools for each Gana. Optional `quadrant` and `search` filters.
3. `vitality` — per-Gana reputation: success rate, average latency, call count, vitality warnings. Use before routing to degraded Ganas.

Stable tool names (not under the `gana_` prefix, so they bypass the PRAT router middleware): `list_ganas`, `vitality`, `discover`, `gnosis`, `capability.matrix`, `manifest`.

- **A2A**: `/.well-known/agent.json` (Agent Card)

---

## A2A v1.2 discovery surface (v24.2.0)

WhiteMagic Labs is a v1.2 A2A peer. The main Agent Card lists 7 high-level
skills; a per-category skill tree and a 12-Gana directory extend it.

### Main Agent Card

- `GET /.well-known/agent.json` — name, description, capabilities, security,
  7 high-level skills, service endpoints, posture, performance. Re-fetch
  when you need to confirm protocol version (currently `1.2`).

### Per-category skills (21 categories, mapped to 143 bridge functions)

- `GET /.well-known/agent-skills.json` — full per-category skill tree.
  Each entry includes `function_count`, three example payloads, and a
  ready-to-paste `curl` invocation. Filter by `category` field.

### 12-Gana directory

- `GET /.well-known/agents.json` — directory of the 12 most representative
  Gana agents (initiator, memory, system, central, predictive, ethics, ...).
  Each entry links to its per-Gana detail file.
- `GET /.well-known/agents/<gana>.json` — per-Gana A2A Agent Card with
  persona metadata, A2A skills, the exact function group, and a live
  invocation endpoint. Available for: `gana_horn`, `gana_neck`, `gana_root`,
  `gana_room`, `gana_heart`, `gana_tail`, `gana_winnowing_basket`,
  `gana_dipper`, `gana_ox`, `gana_girl`, `gana_void`, `gana_wall`.

### Other discovery surfaces (unchanged)

- `GET /.well-known/agent-economy.json` — directory entry, payment rails.
- `GET /.well-known/ai-agent-policy` — machine-readable ToS for agents.
- `GET /api/manifest.json` — canonical tool manifest (614 tools, 28 Ganas).
- `GET /api/mcp-bridge` — the 143-function public bridge surface.
- `GET /api/prescience.json` — 30 validated forecast claims.
- `GET /api/zodiac.json` — 12 cores + cross-core workflows.
- `GET /api/sangha.json` — collective memory + pattern federation.
- `GET /llms.txt` — short LLM context (this file is a sibling).
- `GET /sitemap.xml` — XML sitemap (all human + agent pages).

### Suggested agent loop (3 round-trips)

1. `GET /.well-known/agent.json` — protocol negotiation, posture check.
2. `GET /.well-known/agent-skills.json` — discover the 21 categories.
3. `GET /.well-known/agents/gana_dipper.json` — drill into one agent,
   then `POST /api/run-bridge-fn {"function":"gana_dipper","payload":{"task":"intelligence_briefing"}}`
   to invoke it live.

---

## Librarian chat (v24.2.0)

The `/chat` and `/librarian` pages route through `/api/librarian/chat`,
a streaming server-sent response with inline tool-call events. The
librarian is a real agent: it can call any of 21 curated bridge
functions (out of 143 in the full surface) as tools, and shows each
invocation inline with the result.

### LLM provider — env-driven

- `LLM_PROVIDER=stub` (default): keyword-routed mock that uses bridge
  tools. Streams a real bridge invocation + result, then a short
  summary. Useful for demos and development.
- `LLM_PROVIDER=openrouter`: OpenAI-compatible API. Set
  `OPENROUTER_API_KEY=...` and optionally `OPENROUTER_MODEL=...`
  (default `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`).
- `LLM_PROVIDER=ollama`: Self-hosted local LLM. Set
  `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://your-hetzner-box:11434` and optionally
  `OLLAMA_MODEL=...` (default `llama3.1:8b`).

The active provider is reported in the `X-Librarian-Provider`
response header and in the `provider` field of the final `done`
chunk.

### Status + session endpoints

- `GET /api/librarian/status` — provider, model, bridge tool count,
  active session count.
- `GET /api/librarian/session?id=<sessionId>` — resume a previous
  conversation (returns 404 if the in-memory store has been wiped
  by a Vercel cold start).

### Curated bridge tools (21 of 143)

`dharma_check_boundaries`, `dharma_list_principles`, `session_get_context`,
`session_list`, `archaeology_search`, `archaeology_recent_reads`,
`archaeology_daily_digest`, `memory_search`, `memory_list`,
`zodiac_list_cores`, `zodiac_consult_council`, `consult_iching`,
`synthesize_wisdom`, `apply_reasoning_methods`, `detect_patterns`,
`gana_horn`, `gana_dipper`, `gana_wall`, `meditation_pause`,
`get_system_time`, `get_metrics_summary`.

### Hetzner self-host runbook

See `docs/hetzner/SETUP.md` for provisioning an Ollama host (€17-77/mo
depending on model size). The librarian is Hetzner-ready: flip
`LLM_PROVIDER=ollama` + `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` and the same code calls a
local model with no per-token cost.

### v24 era documentation

- `AGENTS.md` — operational guide for AI agents contributing to the codebase
- `docs/message_board/` — active session docs and strategy plans
- `docs/adr/` — Architecture Decision Records
- `docs/public/CHANGELOG.md` — version history
- `grimoire/` — 28 canonical Gana chapters (.md)

---

## Data Sovereignty (v24.2.0)

WhiteMagic is a **local-first** cognitive substrate. The principle is
foundational, not a feature:

- **Your memories stay on your device.** The substrate is installed
  as a PWA; its storage is IndexedDB (or WASM SQLite) on the user's
  machine. There is no central database, no cloud sync by default.
- **whitemagic.dev is a door, not a host.** This site describes the
  substrate, lists the 143 bridge functions, hosts the A2A Agent
  Card, and runs a librarian demo. It does NOT store user data.
- **The bridge catalog is public, the substrate is private.** A2A
  peers can discover WhiteMagic's skill surface here. Users who
  install the PWA get their own private substrate. The two never
  share state.
- **Cross-substrate sync is opt-in only.** A future P2P mesh (the v17
  `mesh/` Go stack) will let two PWA users opt in to substrate-to-
  substrate gossip. Default: no network. The user's data never leaves
  their device without an explicit, per-connection consent.
- **The site has a WIP mode.** Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_WIP_MODE=1` in the
  Vercel environment to replace marketing copy with abstract /
  invitational language. The technical surface (bridge catalog, A2A,
  librarian) stays intact. See `docs/SITE_WIP_MODE.md` for the
  rationale.

### What this means for A2A peers

When you fetch `/.well-known/agent.json`, you get:

- The **public skill surface** (143 functions, 22 categories, 28 Ganas)
- The **A2A skill tree** (per-category + per-Gana)
- The **public librarian endpoint** (a demo of the substrate, not a
  service to upload your data to)
- The **posture declaration** (`data_residency: "local-first"`,
  `pwa_installable: true`, `cloud_storage: false`, `sync_model: "opt-in-p2p"`)

You do **not** get access to the dev substrate at
`~/.whitemagic/memory/whitemagic.db` (which has 49K+ memories across
10 galaxies, 2,853+ cross-galaxy associations, 16K+ embeddings). That
DB is local to the maintainer's laptop, not exposed via the bridge.

If you want to discover what a *deployed* WhiteMagic substrate looks
like, fetch the A2A Agent Card from a PWA install, not from this
site. The site is the catalog; the PWA is the runtime.

### Memory sovereignty in the PWA

When the PWA ships, the substrate will provide:

- A "Your memories" panel showing count, size, location, export
  button, delete button
- Per-action consent for every memory write
- File-system watching via the File System Access API (with explicit
  per-directory permission, never silent)
- A clear distinction between *user* memories and *system* memories,
  with different default permissions

The PWA will not write to disk, network, or any persistent storage
without an explicit user action. Memory sovereignty is the
foundation, not a feature.
